ddls:
CREATE TABLE "programming_languages" (
  "programming_language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each programming language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the programming language */,
  "language_version" TEXT /* Version of the programming language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("programming_language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_versions" (
  "error_version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error version */,
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the errors table */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the error */,
  "code_diff" TEXT /* Code differences between versions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_versions_error_id FOREIGN KEY ("error_id") REFERENCES errors ("error_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the errors table */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date the comment was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_comments_error_id FOREIGN KEY ("error_id") REFERENCES errors ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_settings" (
  "user_setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user setting */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., preferred programming language) */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the errors table */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytic entry */,
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the errors table */,
  "occurrences" INTEGER /* Number of times the error has occurred */,
  "resolved_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the error has been resolved */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the analytic entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the analytic entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_error_id FOREIGN KEY ("error_id") REFERENCES errors ("error_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "errors" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error */,
  "kind" INTEGER /* Type of error (e.g., syntax error, runtime error) */,
  "function_id" INTEGER /* ID of the function where the error occurred */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* ID of the file where the error occurred */,
  "line" INTEGER /* Line number where the error occurred */,
  "column" INTEGER /* Column number where the error occurred */,
  "programming_language_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the programming_languages table */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Detailed message describing the error */,
  "error_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the error (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the error has been resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_function_id FOREIGN KEY ("function_id") REFERENCES functions ("function_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_programming_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("programming_language_id") REFERENCES programming_languages ("programming_language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES files ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "functions" (
  "function_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each function */,
  "function_name" TEXT /* Name of the function */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the function */,
  "code_snippet" TEXT /* Code snippet representing the function */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("function_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the file */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of file (e.g., Python script, C++ header file) */,
  "code_content" TEXT /* Actual content of the file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, admin) */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing a potential teams table */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "error_id" INTEGER /* ID of the error being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the error */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the error was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "accessed_version" TEXT /* Version of the error that was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_error_id FOREIGN KEY ("error_id") REFERENCES errors ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
